title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation
description: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Learners cumulatively produce rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
